qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default-public explain queries expose MongoDB query execution plans to any user with the application ID
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding describes information disclosure via MongoDB explain queries in the REST API, unrelated to the advisory's LiveQuery CLP bypass vulnerability. The advisory describes missing class-level permission enforcement for LiveQuery subscriptions and event delivery, while this finding describes a default insecure configuration for the allowPublicExplain option in REST API endpoints. Different vulnerable component (LiveQuery vs REST API), different root cause (CLP bypass vs insecure default), different attack surface (WebSocket subscription vs HTTP GET).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a class-level permissions (CLP) bypass in Parse Server's LiveQuery subscription mechanism, where unauthenticated clients can subscribe to LiveQuery-enabled classes and receive real-time events without CLP enforcement. Finding 1 (default-public-explain-disclosure) describes MongoDB explain query disclosure via REST API, which is unrelated to LiveQuery CLP. Neither finding matches the LiveQuery CLP bypass root cause, vulnerable component, or attack vector described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory describes CLP bypass in LiveQuery subscription and event delivery paths
Advisory affected component: LiveQuery real-time subscription system
Advisory impact: Unauthorized access to real-time data streams protected by CLP
Finding 1 vulnerability: MongoDB explain query exposure in REST API (different component, different root cause)
